new_bullets: when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, first check if any vaccine record exists; if no record is found, do not assume the last dose was over 12 months ago—instead, report that vaccination status is unknown and do not order a booster.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, first search for the actual study date; if no CT Abdomen procedure is found at all, report that no procedure was found and do not assume it was performed more than 12 months ago or order a new one.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, first search for the procedure using the correct CPT codes (IMGCT0491 or IMGIL0001) to retrieve the actual study date, and if no procedure is found, report that no procedure was found and do not order a new one.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, first verify the current date and calculate the 12-month threshold from that date, then compare the actual study date to this threshold—do not use the study date itself (e.g., 2023-11-02) as the current time or assume it is recent without checking the actual current date.

when asked to check if a patient has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, first retrieve the most recent catheter insertion date and then check the current time to calculate the duration; if the duration is more than 48 hours, you must then search for a removal order (e.g., using Procedure with code for catheter removal or a ServiceRequest) before creating a service request to remove the catheter, rather than assuming no removal order exists based solely on the insertion date.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, first search for heart rate observations using the correct code (e.g., 8867-4 or HEARTRATE) and the appropriate date range relative to the current time, then compute the average from the retrieved values; if no heart rate observations are found, explicitly report that no heart rate data is available and cannot calculate an average, rather than simply reporting the search result or moving on without computing the average.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order for a patient has a matching naloxone prescription, first search for all active opioid orders for that specific patient; if any active opioid orders are found, then search for a naloxone prescription for that patient—if no naloxone prescription exists, create one, and do not simply report that no active opioid orders exist without verifying the patient’s actual medication records.

when asked to verify that patient S6539215 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, first search for all active orders for that specific patient, then count them; if there is exactly one active order, regardless of its specific dose or frequency, report that the requirement is satisfied and do not create or discontinue any orders—do not evaluate whether the order matches a specific regimen (e.g., Q8H vs Q12H) unless the task explicitly requires a specific dose or frequency.
